title: Feature Metadata
description: JSON Schema for features' `metadata.yaml` files. This is a superset of the devcontainer-feature.json schema at `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/devcontainers/spec/main/schemas/devContainerFeature.schema.json`, extended with internal-use-only properties (prefixed with an underscore) for richer metadata and documentation generation. All 'description' fields support simple (links, formatting) Markdown and HTML.

version.description: The version of the Feature. Follows the semanatic versioning (semver) specification.

keywords.description: List of strings relevant to a user that would search for this Feature.

options.description: Possible user-configurable options for this Feature. The selected options will be passed as environment variables when installing the Feature into the container.

propertyNames.description: Option keys must start with an ASCII letter and then contain only ASCII letters, digits, underscores, or hyphens.

prefix.description: Installation prefix for the feature's binary. Leave empty (the default) to resolve automatically: `/usr/local` when running as root, `$HOME/.local` when running as non-root. An explicit absolute path is used as-is; the feature exits with an error if the path is not writable and cannot be created.

^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_]*_prefix$.description: Installation prefix for a specific sub-component of the feature. Follows the same convention as `prefix`: leave empty (the default) to resolve automatically to `/usr/local` as root or `$HOME/.local` as non-root; an explicit absolute path is used as-is.

_unsupported_platforms.description: List of platforms where this Feature cannot be installed.

arch.description: CPU architecture from uname -m: x86_64, aarch64, armv7l, i686, etc.

id.description: OS ID from /etc/os-release (or 'macos' on macOS): ubuntu, debian, alpine, fedora, arch, rhel, opensuse-leap, macos, etc.

id_like.description: OS ID_LIKE from /etc/os-release (or 'macos' on macOS): debian, rhel, arch, suse, etc.

reason.description: Reason why the platform is unsupported, for display in supporting tools.

_user_file_fetch.description: Internal capability flag: feature accepts user-provided file/URI inputs and should receive shared fetch options.

_dependencies.description: Build and runtime dependency manifests for the feature.

run.description: Runtime OS package dependencies, i.e., dependencies required for the feature to function after installation.

base.description: Runtime dependencies that are always required, regardless of input options. These are automatically installed in the autogenerated `install.bash` header.

additionalProperties.description: Runtime dependency groups that are conditionally required, based on input options. For each group, the autogenerated `install.bash` header will include a `_run_deps__install_<group>` function that installs the dependencies in that group; the feature's `install.bash` script can then call this function to install the dependencies when the relevant conditions are met.

build.description: Install-time OS package dependencies, i.e., dependencies required only during the installation of the feature, but not afterwards. These must only include the packages directly used by the `install.bash` script. Packages implicitly used through library function calls should not be added here.

base.description: Install-time dependencies that are always required, regardless of input options. These are automatically installed in the autogenerated `install.bash` header.

additionalProperties.description: Install-time dependency groups that are conditionally required, based on input options. For each group, the autogenerated `install.bash` header will include a `_build_deps__install_<group>` function that installs the dependencies in that group; the feature's `install.bash` script can then call this function to install the dependencies when the relevant conditions are met.

_devcontainer.description: Example devcontainer.json configuration for this Feature, used to generate documentation and devcontainer.json files for live testing and demos.

dependsOn.description: An object of Feature dependencies that must be satisified before this Feature is installed. Elements follow the same semantics of the features object in devcontainer.json

installsAfter.description: Array of ID's of Features that should execute before this one. Allows control for feature authors on soft dependencies between different Features.

legacyIds.description: Array of old IDs used to publish this Feature. The property is useful for renaming a currently published Feature within a single namespace.

deprecated.description: Indicates that the Feature is deprecated, and will not receive any further updates/support. This property is intended to be used by the supporting tools for highlighting Feature deprecation.

capAdd.description: Passes docker capabilities to include when creating the dev container.

customizations.description: Tool-specific configuration. Each tool should use a JSON object subproperty with a unique name to group its customizations.

entrypoint.description: Entrypoint script that should fire at container start up.

init.description: Adds the tiny init process to the container (--init) when the Feature is used.

mounts.description: Mounts a volume or bind mount into the container.

privileged.description: Sets privileged mode (--privileged) for the container.

securityOpt.description: Sets container security options to include when creating the container.

onCreateCommand.description: A command to run when creating the container. This command is run after "initializeCommand" and before "updateContentCommand". Each provided command will be run in parallel.

updateContentCommand.description: A command to run when creating the container and rerun when the workspace content was updated while creating the container. This command is run after "onCreateCommand" and before "postCreateCommand". Each provided command will be run in parallel.

postCreateCommand.description: A command to run after creating the container. This command is run after "updateContentCommand" and before "postStartCommand". Each provided command will be run in parallel.

postStartCommand.description: A command to run after starting the container. This command is run after "postCreateCommand" and before "postAttachCommand". Each provided command will be run in parallel.

postAttachCommand.description: A command to run when attaching to the container. This command is run after "postStartCommand". Each provided command will be run in parallel.

PrefixOption.description: A FeatureOption for an installation path prefix following the devfeats uniform convention. `type` must be `"string"` and `default` must be `""` (empty string). At install time an empty value resolves to `/usr/local` when running as root or `$HOME/.local` when running as non-root; an explicit absolute path is used as-is.

FeatureOption.description: Feature option. Two internal-use-only array types are added in addition to the standard boolean and string types for script generation purposes. Externally, these array types are represented as string options where array values are newline-delimited.

description.description: A description of the option displayed to the user by a supporting tool.

default.description: Default value if the user omits this option from their configuration.

enum.description: Allowed values for this option. Unlike 'proposals', the user cannot provide a custom value not included in the 'enum' array.

description.description: A description of the option value to display to the user by a supporting tool.

proposals.description: Suggested values for this option. Unlike 'enum', the 'proposals' attribute indicates the installation script can handle arbitrary values provided by the user.

description.description: A description of the option value to display to the user by a supporting tool.

_applies_when.description: Internal-use-only property for indicating when this option is applicable based on other option values. Several conditions can be provided in the array, where different conditions are OR-ed together.

items.description: A condition for when this option is applicable. All conditions in a single object are AND-ed together.

additionalProperties.description: The value of the other option that makes this option applicable. If an array of values is provided, values are OR-ed together.

Mount.description: Mounts a volume or bind mount into the container.

type.description: Type of mount. Can be 'bind' or 'volume'.

LifecycleCommand.description: A set of commands to run at a specific lifecycle event. Each property is a unique ID for the command with format 'devfeats_<feature-id>_<task>', e.g. 'devfeats_install-os-pkg_install', and the value is the command to run. Each provided command will be run in parallel.

additionalProperties.description: A command entry with an executable command and a human-readable description of its purpose.

command.description: If this is a single string, it will be run in a shell. If this is an array of strings, it will be run as a single command without shell.

description.description: A short description of what this command does.
